## `ai/` (16 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `ai/any-density` | medium | on | ✓ | TypeScript `any` density deviates from human baseline — AI agents use `any` as a type-safety escape hatch (Lee, Hassan, Hindle, MSR 2026) |
| `ai/comment-ratio` | medium | on | ✓ | Comment-line ratio deviates from corpus baseline — reductive models skip comments, expansive models over-comment (Rahman et al. 2024, Bisztray et al. 2025) |
| `ai/compression-profile` | low | on | ✓ | NCD/compression profile shows AI boilerplate — gzip ratio > 0.50 + low line-to-line NCD variation (Cilibrasi 2005, Mahoney 1999) |
| `ai/console-debug-storm` | low | on | ✓ | ≥10 console.log/debug/info/warn/error + debugger statements, no structured logger — GitClear 2025 (AI debug noise often left in) |
| `ai/default-react-stack` | low | on | ✓ | File uses ≥3 of the default React stack (Next.js + TanStack Query + Zustand + shadcn/ui) — Sascha 2025 + Nam et al. MSR 2026 |
| `ai/errors-near-eof` | medium | off | ✓ | Unbalanced delimiters or syntax errors cluster near EOF — likely AI token-limit truncation (Yotkova 2026) |
| `ai/fetch-default-overuse` | low | on | ✓ | ≥3 direct fetch() calls with no TanStack Query / SWR / axios — Sascha 2025 (LLMs default to raw fetch even when libs exist) |
| `ai/library-reinvention` | medium | on | ✓ | File reinvents ≥2 common patterns (date picker, form validation, chart, modal, etc.) without importing the canonical library — GitClear 2025 + Cui et al. 2025 |
| `ai/log-rank-histogram` | medium | off | ✓ | GLTR-style log-rank histogram shows AI top-1000 over-concentration — P_top1000 > 0.95 (Gehrmann 2019) |
| `ai/markdown-leakage` | high | on | ✓ | Stray Markdown fence or bare language name at top of file — AI tools leak the fenced-block format into code |
| `ai/renyi-profile` | medium | on | ✓ | Rényi entropy profile shows AI mass concentration — H_2/H_1 > 0.85 + H_∞/H_1 > 0.95 (Rényi 1961, Moslonka 2025) |
| `ai/segment-surprisal-cv` | medium | off | ✓ | Per-segment cross-entropy CV is suspiciously low — Binoculars (Hans 2024): AI text has near-constant per-window entropy |
| `ai/state-default-overuse` | low | on | ✓ | ≥5 useState calls with no useReducer / Zustand / Jotai — Sascha 2025 (LLMs produce 2022-era state patterns) |
| `ai/tailwind-color-overuse` | low | on | ✓ | Tailwind class strings over-represent the default palette (blue-500, slate-50, rounded-lg) — Sascha 2025 + Douglas 2025 |
| `ai/text-like-ratio` | low | on | ✓ | High ratio of prose-like lines embedded in code — AI tools often leave natural-language explanations in source files (Yotkova 2026) |
| `ai/whitespace-regularity` | medium | off | ✓ | Inter-token whitespace distribution is suspiciously uniform — AI code uses regular spacing patterns (Shi et al. 2024, DetectCodeGPT) |

## `component/` (3 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `component/giant-component` | high | on | ✓ | Component file AST complexity exceeds threshold (triggers Context Window Tax). |
| `component/multiple-components-per-file` | medium | on | ✓ | File contains 2+ component definitions — split into separate files. |
| `component/shadcn-prop-mismatch` | high | on | ✓ | Legacy `className` prop assignment on shadcn/ui components whose registry schema explicitly excludes it. |

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `context/import-path-mismatch` | medium | on | ✓ | Import path does not match any allowed prefix from brick.config.json. |

## `cpp/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `cpp/c-style-cast` | low | off | ✓ | C-style cast `(int)x` — use static_cast / reinterpret_cast / const_cast so the kind is greppable. |
| `cpp/magic-numbers` | low | off | ✓ | Magic-number literal in a comparison / return — name it: `constexpr int MAX = 1024;`. |
| `cpp/printf-debug` | low | off | ✓ | printf family or std::cout debug printing — use spdlog / glog / AbslLog for level-controlled output. |
| `cpp/raw-new-delete` | low | off | ✓ | Manual `new` / `delete` pairing — use std::make_unique / std::unique_ptr instead. |
| `cpp/using-namespace-std` | medium | off | ✓ | `using namespace std;` in a header — pollutes every consumer. Use `using std::foo;` or qualify names. |

## `db/` (6 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `db/duplicate-index` | high | off | — | Two CREATE INDEX statements cover the same column list on the same table — drop one. |
| `db/enum-sprawl` | low | off | — | CREATE TYPE … AS ENUM with >${ENUM_VALUES_MAX} values — brittle to extend; prefer a lookup table. |
| `db/missing-fk-index` | high | off | — | Foreign key column without a matching CREATE INDEX in the same file — sequential scan on parent deletes. |
| `db/missing-not-null` | high | off | — | Required-identifier column (id, email, created_at, …) without NOT NULL — silent NULL inserts in production. |
| `db/naming-inconsistency` | low | off | — | snake_case and camelCase identifiers mix within one SQL file — standardize. |
| `db/sql-concat` | high | on | ✓ | Template-literal SQL query with ${...} interpolation — use parameterized queries. |

## `dead/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `dead/dead-branch` | medium | on | ✓ | Literal boolean condition makes one branch statically dead |
| `dead/unreachable` | high | on | ✓ | Statement is unreachable after an unconditional return/throw/break/continue |
| `dead/unused-import` | low | on | ✓ | ES module import is never referenced in the file |
| `dead/unused-local` | low | on | ✓ | Variable is declared but never read after declaration |
| `dead/unused-parameter` | low | on | ✓ | Function parameter is declared but never read |

## `docs/` (3 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `docs/broken-link` | low | on | — | Markdown link target is relative and does not resolve to a file on disk. |
| `docs/stale-function-reference` | medium | on | — | Markdown references an identifier in a calling context (foo()) that is not exported by the project. |
| `docs/stale-package-reference` | medium | on | — | Markdown references a package (npm install / from / require) that is not in package.json. |

## `dup/` (3 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `dup/identical-block` | medium | on | — | Block of >=10 lines is identical across >=2 files (Type-1 clone detector) |
| `dup/near-duplicate` | medium | off | ✓ | Near-duplicate file (Type-2 clone) — token-set Jaccard similarity > 0.7 |
| `dup/structural-clone` | medium | off | ✓ | Structural clone (Type-3) — same shape after identifier canonicalization with possible added/removed statements. Two-stage MinHash: canonical + identifier. |

## `go/` (3 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `go/error-wrap-without-context` | low | off | ✓ | fmt.Errorf wrap without operation context — AI defaults to generic "error: %w" |
| `go/nil-slice-vs-empty` | low | off | ✓ | Variable declared `var x []int` but later assigned `x = []int{}` or `make([]int, n)` — pick one form |
| `go/struct-tag-inconsistency` | low | off | ✓ | Struct fields mix json:"foo" and json:"foo,omitempty" — pick one convention per struct |

## `java/` (11 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `java/arraylist-vs-linkedlist` | low | off | ✓ | new LinkedList<>() — use ArrayList (Effective Java, Item 28) |
| `java/builder-overuse` | low | off | ✓ | @Builder on a class with few fields — plain constructor is simpler |
| `java/empty-catch-block` | medium | off | ✓ | Empty catch block — silently swallows exceptions |
| `java/immutable-collection-preference` | low | off | ✓ | Immutable factory method over-use — prefer mutable when collection will be modified |
| `java/legacy-date-api` | low | off | ✓ | Legacy java.util.Date / Calendar — use java.time (JSR-310) from Java 8+ |
| `java/optional-overuse` | low | off | ✓ | Optional chain over-use — null check or Objects.requireNonNull is faster |
| `java/raw-type-overuse` | low | off | ✓ | Raw type usage (List, Map, Set) — use generics (Effective Java, Item 23) |
| `java/stream-overuse` | low | off | ✓ | Stream API chain over-use — for-loop is faster for simple transformations |
| `java/string-concat-loop` | low | off | ✓ | String concatenation in a loop — use StringBuilder |
| `java/system-out-println` | low | off | ✓ | System.out.println in production code — use a logger (SLF4J, Log4j, etc.) |
| `java/verbose-javadoc` | low | off | ✓ | Excessive Javadoc tags on trivial methods — over-documentation is an AI fingerprint |

## `kotlin/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `kotlin/coroutine-global-scope` | medium | off | ✓ | GlobalScope.launch/async/runBlocking — bypasses structured concurrency. Use viewModelScope / lifecycleScope / coroutineScope {}. |
| `kotlin/data-class-defaults-overuse` | low | off | ✓ | data class with 3+ literal defaults — AI defaults every field rather than only the genuinely optional ones |
| `kotlin/object-singleton-misuse` | medium | off | ✓ | Kotlin object singleton holding mutable `var` state — hidden shared state. Use DI or a repository. |
| `kotlin/println-debug` | low | off | ✓ | println(...) in production code — use Timber, android.util.Log, or an SLF4J facade |
| `kotlin/string-concat-loop` | low | off | ✓ | String concatenation in a loop — use buildString { ... } or a StringBuilder |

## `layout/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `layout/forced-layout` | medium | off | ✓ | Repetitive structural nesting defaulting to `flex flex-col gap-4`. |
| `layout/gap-monopoly` | medium | on | ✓ | A singular uniform gap configuration dominates the project space. |
| `layout/math-element-uniformity` | medium | on | ✓ | Interactive element counts are suspiciously uniform (max/min ≤ 3) |
| `layout/math-grid-uniformity` | high | on | ✓ | grid-cols-N tokens show low entropy — AI uses one column count everywhere, humans vary |
| `layout/spacing-grid` | medium | on | — | Spacing values outside the project scale |

## `logic/` (14 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `logic/bayesian-conditional` | high | off | ✓ | Calibrated Bayesian posterior P(AI \| fired_rules) ≥ 0.7 — peer-reviewed naive-Bayes rule ensemble (Bento et al. 2024, *Neurocomputing*) |
| `logic/boundary-violation` | high | on | ✓ | Server-side data-layer / DB import leaked into a client component, or a client React hook used inside a server file (RSC boundary violation) |
| `logic/ghost-defensive` | medium | on | ✓ | Defensive code for impossible cases |
| `logic/heaps-deviation` | medium | on | — | File vocabulary growth (Heaps λ) deviates significantly from corpus baseline — peer-reviewed LLM indicator (Christ et al. EMNLP 2025) |
| `logic/key-prop-missing` | high | on | ✓ | Items in a list missing a React key |
| `logic/math-any-density` | high | on | ✓ | `: any` density ≥ 3 per 100 lines — AI sprinkling of `any` types |
| `logic/math-console-log-storm` | high | on | ✓ | ≥5 console.logs clustered in a single window — AI debug-spraying pattern |
| `logic/math-gini-class-usage` | high | on | ✓ | Gini coefficient of class usage ≥ 0.5 — a few classes dominate (AI defaults to a tiny vocabulary) |
| `logic/math-variable-name-entropy` | high | on | — | Identifier names show low Shannon entropy — AI defaults to a small vocabulary of generic names |
| `logic/optimistic-no-rollback` | high | on | ✓ | Optimistic UI update without rollback error handling. |
| `logic/qwik-hook-leak` | high | off | ✓ | React hook inside a Qwik component |
| `logic/reactive-hook-soup` | medium | on | ✓ | `useEffect` chains manually syncing local state. |
| `logic/zipf-slope-anomaly` | medium | on | — | File identifier rank-frequency distribution (Zipf s) deviates from corpus baseline — peer-reviewed LLM indicator |
| `logic/zombie-state` | medium | on | ✓ | useState bindings that are never read |

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `perf/cls-image` | low | on | — | Image missing explicit dimensions |
| `perf/css-bloat` | low | on | — | Duplicate raw style blocks or Tailwind token strings repeated >5 times across distinct files. |
| `perf/halstead-anomaly` | medium | on | ✓ | Component Halstead volume per LOC is below corpus baseline — likely AI boilerplate with limited vocabulary. |

## `product/` (2 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `product/terminology-drift` | medium | on | ✓ | Three or more semantically-similar component names differ (e.g. PostList/ArticleList/NewsList on the "List" suffix, or PostList/PostDetail/PostCard on the "Post" prefix). AI agents pick slightly different words each invocation; pick one and standardize. |
| `product/ux-pattern-fragmentation` | medium | on | ✓ | Too many distinct UX patterns per category (e.g. 5 modal implementations, 4 confirmation dialogs). Pick one canonical pattern per category and remove the rest. |

## `rust/` (4 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `rust/stringly-typed` | medium | on | ✓ | String / &str parameter where a typed enum exists in the same file |
| `rust/todo-macro` | medium | on | ✓ | todo!() / unimplemented!() macro invocation in production code |
| `rust/unused-pub-fn` | low | on | ✓ | Public function in a Rust file that has no in-file references |
| `rust/unwrap-in-production` | medium | on | ✓ | .unwrap() / .expect() called outside of #[cfg(test)] / #[test] scope |

## `security/` (11 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `security/dangerous-cors` | medium | on | ✓ | Wildcard or reflective CORS configuration: 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' / origin: '*' / origin: true. |
| `security/eval` | high | on | — | eval() / new Function() / window.eval() — RCE vector when the argument is attacker-controlled (OWASP A03:2021) |
| `security/exposed-env-var` | high | on | — | Secret-looking env var name exposed via a client-bundle prefix (NEXT_PUBLIC_*, VITE_*, etc.). Inlined into every browser build. |
| `security/fail-open-auth` | high | on | ✓ | Authentication or middleware handler returns early (true/next) based on a development-environment check — auth bypass that ships to production. |
| `security/hardcoded-secret` | high | on | ✓ | String literal assigned to a sensitive identifier or matching a known provider key prefix (sk-, AKIA, ghp_, etc.). |
| `security/localstorage-token` | high | on | — | Auth token stored in localStorage — vulnerable to XSS exfiltration (OWASP A03:2021). |
| `security/missing-auth-check` | medium | on | — | Server route handler (Next.js route.ts / pages/api / Express route) with no detectable authentication or authorization check. |
| `security/public-admin-route` | medium | on | — | Privileged route (path contains /admin, /internal, /debug, etc.) without an additional role/permission check. |
| `security/sql-construction` | high | on | ✓ | SQL query built by string concatenation or template interpolation — use parameterized queries / prepared statements instead. |
| `security/target-blank-no-noopener` | medium | on | — | Link with target="_blank" missing rel="noopener" — window.opener can |
| `security/unsafe-html-render` | high | on | — | dangerouslySetInnerHTML used with a non-literal value (variable, template literal, expression) — unsanitized HTML injection risk. |

## `swift/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `swift/fatal-error-thrown` | high | off | ✓ | fatalError / preconditionFailure in production — crashes unconditionally. Return an error or a typed default. |
| `swift/force-unwrap` | medium | off | ✓ | Swift force-unwrap (as!, var!.prop, try!) — silently crashes if the value is nil. Use as? / guard let / try?. |
| `swift/implicitly-unwrapped-optional` | low | off | ✓ | Implicitly unwrapped optional (var x: Type!) — declare as Type? and unwrap with guard let. |
| `swift/print-debug` | low | off | ✓ | print(...) in production Swift — use Logger (os.log) for level-controlled output |
| `swift/strong-self-capture` | medium | off | ✓ | Closure captures `self` strongly — creates a retain cycle. Use `[weak self]` or `[unowned self]`. |

## `test/` (4 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `test/duplicate-setup` | medium | on | ✓ | Repeated beforeEach/beforeAll/setupServer blocks within the same file. Extract to a helper. |
| `test/fake-placeholder` | high | on | ✓ | Object-literal fixture uses textbook placeholder data (e.g. 'John Doe', 'test@test.com', id: 1). |
| `test/missing-edge-case` | high | off | ✓ | Production branch (if/else, try/catch, ternary, ?? fallback) has no corresponding test. Opt-in via testIntelligence.missingEdgeCase. |
| `test/weak-assertion` | medium | on | ✓ | Assertion does not test a specific value (e.g. expect(x).toBeDefined(), expect(x).toBeTruthy(), expect(x).toBe(x)). |

## `ts/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `ts/enum-vs-as-const` | low | off | ✓ | Uses `enum` — modern TS prefers `as const` objects |
| `ts/excessive-type-assertion` | low | off | ✓ | Function with >3 `as` type assertions — likely AI fighting the type system |
| `ts/import-type-misuse` | low | off | ✓ | Inline `import { type X }` — prefer `import type { X }` for clarity |
| `ts/never-vs-unknown` | low | off | ✓ | Return type `: never` but body has no throw / loop / exit — likely AI misuse |
| `ts/optional-chain-overuse` | low | off | ✓ | Optional chaining (?.) used 5+ times in a single chain — AI tends to chain rather than narrow |

## `typo/` (6 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `typo/calc-fontsize` | medium | off | — | Mathematical `calc()` assigned to `font-size` lacking a design token baseline. |
| `typo/calc-raw-px` | high | on | — | calc() with raw px units in style props |
| `typo/clamp-offscale` | medium | off | — | Responsive typography `clamp()` sizes deviating >20% from baseline design scale. |
| `typo/math-button-label-uniformity` | medium | on | ✓ | Button text lengths have suspiciously low variance — AI writes "Get started", "Sign up", "Learn more" |
| `typo/math-cta-vocabulary` | medium | off | ✓ | CTA button text falls back to a small AI-default vocabulary in ≥80% of buttons |
| `typo/placeholder-text` | low | on | — | Placeholder text contains dev/AI defaults (Lorem ipsum, Enter text here, TODO, etc.) — unfinished UI. |

## `visual/` (13 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `visual/arbitrary-escape` | medium | on | ✓ | Bracket-notation Tailwind values (e.g. `p-[13px]`, `bg-[#7c3aed]`) — AI agents reach for arbitrary escapes instead of design tokens (Refactoring UI; Mäntylä 2003) |
| `visual/clamp-soup` | high | off | ✓ | Unrealistic, un-aliased viewport configurations in inline styles (e.g., `clamp(1rem, 10vw, 5rem)`). |
| `visual/generic-centering` | low | off | ✓ | Superset of centering tokens (`flex`, `items-center`, `justify-center`, `min-h-screen`). Allows max 1 per file. |
| `visual/inline-style-dominance` | medium | on | ✓ | File uses inline styles for 3+ distinct properties — prefer className utilities. |
| `visual/math-color-cluster` | high | on | ✓ | Hex colors cluster in a tight hue range (≤90° spread) — AI default-palette tell |
| `visual/math-default-font` | high | on | ✓ | UI uses framework default font (Inter/system) with no custom font import — strong AI signature |
| `visual/math-font-entropy` | high | on | ✓ | Text-size tokens show low entropy — AI uses 2–3 sizes, humans use 4–6 |
| `visual/math-gradient-hue-rotation` | high | off | ✓ | Gradients cluster around small hue rotations (≤30°), the AI violet-to-fuchsia tell |
| `visual/math-rounded-entropy` | high | on | ✓ | Border-radius tokens show abnormally low entropy — AI sticks to lg/xl/full |
| `visual/math-spacing-entropy` | medium | on | ✓ | Numeric spacing tokens show abnormally low entropy — AI tends to repeat the same 2–3 spacing values |
| `visual/naturalness-anomaly` | medium | on | ✓ | Identifier stream has unusually low distinct-token ratio (<30%) — AI-default-naming signature (Hindle 2012 §4.3). |
| `visual/radius-scale-violation` | medium | on | — | Border-radius utility uses an arbitrary value (e.g. `rounded-[7px]`) outside the declared design-system scale. |
| `visual/spacing-scale-violation` | medium | on | — | Spacing utility uses an arbitrary value (e.g. `p-[13px]`) outside the declared design-system scale. |

## `wcag/` (5 rules)

| Rule | Severity | Default | AI-specific | Description |
|------|----------|:-------:|:-----------:|-------------|
| `wcag/dragging-movements` | medium | on | — | Draggable element without a pointer/keyboard alternative. |
| `wcag/focus-appearance` | high | on | — | Inject a global focus-ring CSS block (`*:focus-visible { outline: ... }`) — WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) |
| `wcag/focus-obscured` | low | on | — | Sticky/fixed positioning that hides focused siblings. |
| `wcag/missing-alt` | medium | on | — | <img> element is missing an `alt` attribute (WCAG 2.1 SC 1.1.1, Level A). |
| `wcag/target-size` | high | off | — | Interactive element smaller than 24×24 px |

## Glossary

- **Default** — whether the rule runs out of the box. Rules marked `off` are `defaultOff: true` in [../src/rules/signal-strength.json](../src/rules/signal-strength.json) (typically INVERTED, NOISY, or DORMANT calibration verdict) and require explicit opt-in via `rules: { '<id>': 'medium' }` in `slopbrick.config.mjs`.
- **AI-specific** — the rule only fires on patterns introduced (or disproportionately introduced) by AI coding agents. Rules without the ✓ may still correlate with AI authorship but the underlying pattern is not AI-unique.
- **Severity** — see [scoring-runbook.md](./scoring-runbook.md) for the per-severity weight in PR Slop Score.

## Regenerating this catalog

This file is **auto-generated** by [scripts/generate-rule-catalog.ts](../scripts/generate-rule-catalog.ts) which reads each rule file in [../src/rules/](../src/rules/) and [../src/rules/signal-strength.json](../src/rules/signal-strength.json) for the `defaultOff` flag.

If you add or change a rule, regenerate the registry (which also regenerates this catalog):

```bash
pnpm generate:rules
```

This runs automatically before `pnpm build` and `pnpm test` via the existing `prebuild` / pre-`test` chain.
